title.en: How to keep the conversation FLOWING in Korean

description.en: Don't let the silence get awkward! Master the art of active listening and the phrases like "그래서요?" that make people want to keep talking to you.
description.ko: 대화가 끊길까 봐 걱정될 때! 상대방의 이야기에 호기심을 보이며 더 많은 이야기를 이끌어내는 '마법의 추임새' 를 배워봅니다.

tags: Situation. Social. Active Listening. Curiosity. Conversation. Season 2

content.speaker: John
content.text: Mina, I've noticed that some people are just magnets for conversation.

content.speaker: John
content.text: They don't even talk much, but everyone keeps talking to them! What's their secret?

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 맞아요. 그런 분들은 이야기를 잘하는 게 아니라 리액션을 잘하는 거예요!
content.translation: Right. Those people aren't good at talking, they're good at reacting!

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 상대방의 말에 호기심을 가지고 계속 질문을 던지는 리액션. 한국어로는 '추임새' 라고 하죠.
content.translation: The reaction of asking questions with curiosity about what the other person is saying is called 'Chuh-im-sae' in Korean.

content.speaker: John
content.text: Ah, like verbal nods that say "Tell me more!"?

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 정확해요! 오늘은 대화를 끊이지 않게 만드는 마법의 단어들을 배워볼게요.
content.translation: Exactly! Today, we'll learn the magic words that keep a conversation going.

content.speaker: John
content.text: 그래서요? This is like "And then?" or "So?" right?

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 네! 상대방의 다음 이야기가 너무 궁금하다는 걸 보여주는 가장 좋은 표현이에요.
content.translation: Yes! It's the best expression to show that you're very curious about the other person's next story.

insight.title: 그래서요?
insight.usage_tip: '그래서(And so/Therefore)' + '요(Polite ending)'. This simple phrase encourages the speaker to continue their story.

content.speaker: John
content.text: 그래서요? 그다음엔 어떻게 됐어요?
content.translation: And then? What happened after that?

content.speaker: John
content.text: What if I want to show that I'm surprised by what they said?

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 그럴 땐 '진짜요?' 혹은 '우와 대박!' 이라고 해보세요.
content.translation: In that case, try saying "진짜요?" or "우와, 대박!".

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 이건 내 감정을 실어서 상대방의 이야기에 집중하고 있다는 걸 보여주는 리액션이에요.
content.translation: This is a reaction that shows you're concentrating on their story by putting your emotions into it.

insight.title: 진짜요?
insight.usage_tip: '진짜(Real/Truth)' + '요(Polite ending)'. One of the most common reactions in Korea to show engagement and surprise.

content.speaker: John
content.text: Okay, let's jump in. Mina is telling me a crazy story about something that happened on her vacation.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 제가 이번 휴가 때 산에 갔는데 거기서 정말 커다란 곰을 만난 거 있죠!
content.translation: John, I went to the mountains this vacation, and I met a really huge bear there!

content.speaker: John
content.text: 우와 진짜요? 산에서 곰을 만났다고요?
content.translation: Wow, really? You met a bear in the mountains?

insight.title: 진짜요?
insight.usage_tip: Repeating the key part of their sentence ("곰을 만났다고요?") makes your reaction sound even more authentic.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 네! 너무 놀라서 가만히 서 있었는데 곰이 저를 빤히 쳐다보는 거예요.
content.translation: Yes! I was so surprised that I stood still, and the bear was staring right at me.

content.speaker: John
content.text: 세상에! 그래서요? 그다음엔 어떻게 됐어요?
content.translation: Oh my god! And then? What happened after that?

insight.title: 그래서요?
insight.usage_tip: Use this when the story reaches a climax to show you're on the edge of your seat.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 다행히 곰이 그냥 지나갔어요. 정말 죽는 줄 알았어요!
content.translation: Fortunately, the bear just passed by. I really thought I was going to die!

content.speaker: John
content.text: 와 정말 무사해서 다행이에요!
content.translation: Wow, I'm glad you're safe!

content.speaker: John
content.text: It's amazing! I didn't say much, but Mina told me the whole story because of my reactions.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 맞아요. "그래서요?" 라는 한마디가 상대방에게는 "네 이야기가 정말 재미있어!" 라는 응원이 되거든요.
content.translation: Right. The word "그래서요?" becomes an encouragement to the other person, saying "Your story is really interesting!"

content.speaker: John
content.text: Let's review our "curiosity" reactions one last time.

content.speaker: John
content.text: 그래서요?
content.translation: And then? / So?

content.speaker: John
content.text: 진짜요?
content.translation: Really?

content.speaker: John
content.text: 그다음엔 어떻게 됐어요?
content.translation: What happened next?

content.speaker: John
content.text: 우와 대박!
content.translation: Wow, amazing! / No way!

content.speaker: John
content.text: 세상에!
content.translation: Oh my goodness!

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 여러분도 오늘 친구의 이야기를 들을 때 눈을 맞추며 "그래서요?" 라고 물어봐 주세요.
content.translation: Everyone, when you listen to a friend's story today, look them in the eye and ask, "And then?"

content.speaker: John
content.text: That's all for today. See you in the next episode.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 다음에 또 만나요. 안녕~!
content.translation: See you again next time. Bye~!

vocabulary.example: 국악이나 대화에서 추임새를 잘 넣으면 흥이 나요. (In traditional Korean music or conversation, good interjections make it more exciting.)
대화의 흐름을 끊지 않는 것이 중요해요. (It's important not to break the flow of the conversation.)
아이가 사탕을 빤히 쳐다보고 있어요. (The child is staring fixedly at the candy.)
비가 많이 왔지만 다행히 늦지 않았어요. (It rained a lot, but fortunately, I wasn't late.)
여행을 무사히 마치고 돌아왔습니다. (I've returned after finishing the trip safely.)
친구의 도전을 진심으로 응원해요. (I sincerely support my friend's challenge.)
